briefdescription.para: Synchronization Jump Width.
Time Segment 1.
Time Segment 2.
Synchronization Jump Width for Data Phase.
Time Segment 1 for Data Phase.
Time Segment 2 for Data Phase.
Configuration mode.
Normal mode.
Loop Back mode.
Sleep mode.
Snoop mode.
Auto Bus-Off Recovery.
Starut Bus-Off Recovery.
Protocol Exception mode.
Disable Auto Retransmission mode.
Bus-Off Recovery Mode.
Selection for RX Fifo 0.
Selection for RX Fifo 1.

sectiondef.header: Callback identifiers used as parameters to XCanFd_SetHandler()

briefdescription.para: Handler type for frame sending interrupt.
Handler type for frame reception interrupt.
Handler type for error interrupt.
Handler type for all other interrupts.
by using protection macros
This macro checks if the transmission is complete.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para:
- TRUE if the transmission is done (completed).
FALSE if the transmission is not completed.

briefdescription.para: This macro calculates CAN message identifier value given identifier field values.

detaileddescription.para:
StandardId
contains Standard Message ID value.
SubRemoteTransReq
contains Substitute Remote Transmission Request value.
IdExtension
contains Identifier Extension value.
ExtendedId
contains Extended Message ID value.
RemoteTransReq
contains Remote Transmission Request value.
Message Identifier value.
C-Style signature: u32 XCanFd_CreateIdValue(u32 StandardId, u32 SubRemoteTransReq, u32 IdExtension, u32 ExtendedId, u32 RemoteTransReq); Read the CAN specification for meaning of each parameter.

briefdescription.para: This macro calculates value for Data Length Code register given Data Length Code value with EDL set to 1(Only Can FD frames).

parameterdescription.para: indicates Data Length Code value.

simplesect.para: Value that can be assigned to Data Length Code register.
C-Style signature: u32 XCanFd_Create_CanFD_DlcValue(u32 DataLengCode); Read the CAN specification for meaning of Data Length Code.

briefdescription.para: This macro calculates value for Data Length Code register given Data Length Code value with EDL set to 1(Only Can FD frames) and Setting the BRS.

detaileddescription.para:
DataLengCode
indicates Data Length Code value.
Value that can be assigned to Data Length Code register.
C-Style signature: u32 XCanFd_Create_CanFD_Dlc_BrsValue(u32 DataLengCode); Read the CAN specification for meaning of Data Length Code.

briefdescription.para: This macro calculates value for Data Length Code register given Data Length Code value i.e Only Stand.

detaileddescription.para: . Can frames.

DataLengCode
indicates Data Length Code value.
Value that can be assigned to Data Length Code register.
C-Style signature: u32 XCanFd_CreateDlcValue(u32 DataLengCode); Read the CAN specification for meaning of Data Length Code.

briefdescription.para: This macro checks whether Particular Buffer is Transmitted or not.

detaileddescription.para: Transmit Ready Request Register gives which Buffer is transmitted if you trigger the Buffer1 then TRR Reg : 0x00000001 After transmission Core clears the bit TRR Reg : 0x00000000

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.
is the buffer where driver has written user data\

simplesect.para:
- TRUE if the device is busy and NOT ready to accept writes to AFIR and AFMR.
FALSE if the device is ready to accept writes to AFIR and AFMR.

briefdescription.para: This macro initializes CurrentBuffer[32] to zeros.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This macro Returns the TXBUFFER ID Offset.

parameterdescription.para: is the Buffer number to locate the FIFO Index

briefdescription.para: This macro Returns the TXBUFFER DLC Offset.

parameterdescription.para: is the Buffer number to locate the FIFO

briefdescription.para: This macro Returns the TXBUFFER DW Offset.

parameterdescription.para: is the Buffer number to locate the FIFO

briefdescription.para: This macro Returns the TX Event Buffer ID Offset.

parameterdescription.para: is the Buffer number to locate the TXE FIFO Index

simplesect.para: This API is meant to be used with IP with CanFD 2.0 spec support only.

briefdescription.para: This macro Returns the TX Event Buffer DLC Offset.

parameterdescription.para: is the Buffer number to locate the FIFO

simplesect.para: This API is meant to be used with IP with CanFD 2.0 spec support only.

briefdescription.para: This macro Returns the RXBUFFER ID Offset.

parameterdescription.para: is the Buffer number to locate the FIFO

briefdescription.para: This macro Returns the RXBUFFER DLC Offset.

parameterdescription.para: is the Buffer number to locate the FIFO

briefdescription.para: This macro Returns the RXBUFFER DW Offset.

parameterdescription.para: is the Buffer number to locate the FIFO

briefdescription.para: This macro Returns the RXBUFFER ID Offset for FIFO 1.

parameterdescription.para: is the Buffer number to locate the FIFO

simplesect.para: This API is meant to be used with IP with CanFD 2.0 spec support only.

briefdescription.para: This macro Returns the RXBUFFER DLC Offset for FIFO 1.

parameterdescription.para: is the Buffer number to locate the FIFO

simplesect.para: This API is meant to be used with IP with CanFD 2.0 spec support only.

briefdescription.para: This macro Returns the RXBUFFER DW Offset for FIFO 1.

parameterdescription.para: is the Buffer number to locate the FIFO

simplesect.para: This API is meant to be used with IP with CanFD 2.0 spec support only.

briefdescription.para: This macro Returns the RCS Register Offset.

parameterdescription.para: is to locate RCS Registers

briefdescription.para: This macro Returns the AFMR Register Offset.

parameterdescription.para: is the Index number of Mask Register

briefdescription.para: This macro Returns the AFIDR Registger Offset.

parameterdescription.para: is the Index of Id Register

briefdescription.para: This macro Returns the MAILBOX MODE RXMASK Offset.

parameterdescription.para: is the Buffer number to locate the FIFO

briefdescription.para: This macro Returns the MAILBOX MODE ID Offset.

parameterdescription.para: is the Buffer number to locate the FIFO

briefdescription.para: This macro Returns Design mode 1- Mailbox 0- Sequential.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function resets the CAN device.

detaileddescription.para: Calling this function resets the device immediately, and any pending transmission or reception is terminated at once. Both Object Layer and Transfer Layer are reset. This function does not reset the Physical Layer. All registers are reset to the default values, and no previous status will be restored. TX FIFO, RX FIFO and TX High Priority Buffer are also reset.

When a reset is required due to an internal error, the driver notifies the upper layer software of this need through the error status code or interrupts The upper layer software is responsible for calling this Reset function and then re-configuring the device.
The CAN device will be in Configuration Mode immediately after this function returns.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function reads Error Status value from Error Status Register (ESR).

detaileddescription.para: Use the XCANFD_ESR_* constants defined in xcanfd_hw.h to interpret the returned value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: The 32-bit value read from Error Status Register.

briefdescription.para: This function returns Status value from Status Register (SR).

detaileddescription.para: Use the XCANFD_SR_* constants defined in xcanfd_hw.h to interpret the returned value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: The 32-bit value read from Status Register.

briefdescription.para: This function clears Error Status bit(s) previously set in Error Status Register (ESR).

detaileddescription.para: Use the XCANFD_ESR_* constants defined in xcanfd_hw.h to create the value to pass in. If a bit was cleared in Error Status Register before this function is called, it will not be touched.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.
is he 32-bit mask used to clear bits in Error Status Register. Multiple XCANFD_ESR_* values could be 'OR'ed to clear multiple bits

briefdescription.para: This function returns the Tranceive delay comensation Offset.

detaileddescription.para: This function can call when user sends multiple Buffers using Addto_Queue() and XCanFd_Send_Queue().

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function Clears Time Stamp Counter Value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function returns Time Stamp Counter Value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This routine returns the Rx water Mark threshold Value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance.

briefdescription.para: This routine returns enabled interrupt(s).

detaileddescription.para: Use the XCANFD_IXR_* constants defined in xcanfd_hw.h to interpret the returned value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This routine returns interrupt status read from Interrupt Status Register.

detaileddescription.para: Use the XCANFD_IXR_* constants defined in xcanfd_hw.h to interpret the returned value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: The value stored in Interrupt Status Register.

briefdescription.para: This routine returns Number of RCS registers to access because in Mail box mode user can configure 48,32,16 Rx Buffers.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: The value stored in Interrupt Status Register.

briefdescription.para: This routine returns Number of RxBuffers user can Design RxBuffers as 48,32,16.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: The value stored in Interrupt Status Register.

briefdescription.para: This routine returns Number with right most bit set from the target input value.

parameterdescription.para: is target value.

simplesect.para: Number with right most bit set from the target value.

briefdescription.para: Callback type for frame sending and reception interrupts.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked.

briefdescription.para: Callback type for error interrupt.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked.
is a bit mask indicating the cause of the error. Its value equals 'OR'ing one or more XCANFD_ESR_* values defined in xcan_l.h

briefdescription.para: Callback type for all kinds of interrupts except sending frame interrupt, receiving frame interrupt, and error interrupt.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked.
is a bit mask indicating the pending interrupts. Its value equals 'OR'ing one or more XCANFD_IXR_* defined in xcanfd_hw.h

briefdescription.para: Config table.
This routine initializes a specific XCanFd instance/driver.

detaileddescription.para: This function should only be used when no Virtual Memory support is needed.

This initialization entails:
Search for device configuration given the device ID.
Initialize Base Address field of the XCanFd structure using the device
address in the found device configuration.
Populate all other data fields in the XCanFd structure
Reset the device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.
is the pointer to XCanFd_Config instance
is the base address of CANFD

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS if initialization was successful
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if device configuration information was not found for a device with the supplied device ID.

briefdescription.para: This routine releases resources of XCanFd instance/driver.

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS if node release was successful
XST_FAILURE or an error code or a reason code if node release was fail

briefdescription.para: This routine returns current operation mode the CAN device is in.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para:
- XCANFD_MODE_CONFIG if the device is in Configuration Mode.
XCANFD_MODE_SLEEP if the device is in Sleep Mode.

listitem.para: XCANFD_MODE_NORMAL if the device is in Normal Mode.
XCANFD_MODE_LOOPBACK if the device is in Loop Back Mode.
XCANFD_MODE_SNOOP if the device is in Snoop Mode.
XCANFD_MODE_BR if the device is in Bus-Off recovery Mode.

XCANFD_MODE_PEE if the device is in Protocol Exception Event mode.
None.

briefdescription.para: This function allows the CAN device to enter one of the following operation modes:

listitem.para: Configuration Mode: Pass in parameter XCANFD_MODE_CONFIG
Sleep Mode: Pass in parameter XCANFD_MODE_SLEEP
Normal Mode: Pass in parameter XCANFD_MODE_NORMAL
Loop Back Mode: Pass in parameter XCANFD_MODE_LOOPBACK
Snoop Mode: Pass in Parameter XCANFD_MODE_SNOOP
Auto Bus-Off Recovery Mode: Pass in Parameter XCANFD_MODE_ABR
Start Bus-Off Recovery Mode: Pass in Parameter XCANFD_MODE_SBR
Protocol Exception Event Mode: Pass in Parameter XCANFD_MODE_PEE
Disable AutoRetransmission Mode: Pass in Parameter XCANFD_MODE_DAR

detaileddescription.para: Read xcanfd.h and device specification for detailed description of each operation mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.
specify which operation mode to enter.Valid value is any of XCANFD_MODE_* defined in xcanfd.h. Please note no multiple modes could be entered at the same time.

simplesect.para: This function does NOT ensure CAN device enters the specified operation mode before returns the control to the caller. The caller is responsible for checking current operation mode using XCanFd_GetMode().

briefdescription.para: This function reads Receive and Transmit error counters.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.
will contain Receive Error Counter value after this function returns.
will contain Transmit Error Counter value after this function returns.

briefdescription.para: This function sends a CAN/CANFD Frame.

detaileddescription.para: This function first checks whether free buffer is there or not.if free buffer is there the user data will be written into the free buffer.otherwise it returns error code immediately. This function does not wait for the given frame being sent to CAN bus.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.
is a pointer to a 32-bit aligned buffer containing the CAN frame to be sent.
is the buffer where the user data has been written and it is updated by driver.

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS if TX FIFO was not full and the given frame was written into the FIFO;
XST_FIFO_NO_ROOM if there is no room in the TX FIFO for the given frame

briefdescription.para: This routine enables the acceptance filters.

detaileddescription.para: Up to 32 filters can be enabled.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.

specifies which filter(s) to enable. Use any XCANFD_AFR_UAF*_MASK to enable one filter, and "Or" multiple XCANFD_AFR_UAF*_MASK values if multiple filters need to be enabled. Any filter not specified in this parameter will keep its previous enable/disable setting.

simplesect.para: In Sequential Mode, in order to receive data, we need to enable these filters. if we want to make filtration i.e Id match then we need to set the Id value in AFR Id register.

briefdescription.para: This routine disables the acceptance filters.

detaileddescription.para: 32 filters can be disabled.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.

specifies which filter(s) to disable. Use any XCANFD_AFR_UAF*_MASK to disable one filter, and "Or" multiple XCANFD_AFR_UAF*_MASK values if multiple filters need to be disabled. Any filter not specified in this parameter will keep its previous enable/disable setting. If all acceptance filters are disabled then all received frames are stored in the RX FIFO.

briefdescription.para: This function returns enabled acceptance filters.

detaileddescription.para: Use XCANFD_AFR_UAF*_MASK defined in xcanfd_hw.h to interpret the returned value. If no acceptance filters are enabled then all received frames are stored in the RX FIFO.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: The value stored in Acceptance Filter Register.

Acceptance Filter Register is an optional register in Xilinx CAN device If it is NOT existing in the device, this function should NOT be used.Calling this function in this case will cause an assertion failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (XCanFd *InstancePtr, u32 FilterIndex, u32 MaskValue, u32 IdValue)
memberdef.name: XCanFd_AcceptFilterSet

briefdescription.para: This function sets values to the Acceptance Filter Mask Register (AFMR) and Acceptance Filter ID Register (AFIR) for the specified Acceptance Filter.

detaileddescription.para: Use XCANFD_IDR_* defined in xcanfd_hw.h to create the values to set the filter. Read xcanfd.h and device specification for details.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.

defines which Acceptance Filter Mask and ID Register to set. Use any single XCANFD_AFR_UAF*_MASK value.ranges from 1
32
is the value to write to the chosen Acceptance Filter Mask Register.
is the value to write to the chosen Acceptance Filter ID Register.

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS if the values were set successfully.
XST_FAILURE if the given filter was not disabled, or the CAN device was not ready to accept writes to AFMR and AFIR.
Acceptance Filter Mask and ID Registers are optional registers in Xilinx XCanFd device.if they are not configured then device will receive data with any ID.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the values of the Acceptance Filter Mask and ID Register for the specified Acceptance Filter.

detaileddescription.para: Use XCANFD_IDR_* defined in xcanfd_hw.h to interpret the values. Read xcanfd.h and device specification for details.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.
defines which Acceptance Filter Mask Register to get Mask and ID from. Use any single XCANFD_FILTER_* value.
will store the Mask value read from the chosen Acceptance Filter Mask Register after this function returns.
will store the ID value read from the chosen Acceptance Filter ID Register after this function returns.

simplesect.para: Acceptance Filter Mask and ID Registers are optional registers in Xilinx CAN device. If they are NOT existing in the device, this function should NOT be used. Calling this function in this case will cause an assertion failure.

briefdescription.para: Looks up the device configuration based on the unique device ID/BaseAddress.

detaileddescription.para: The XCanFd_ConfigTable[] contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration found or NULL if the specified device ID/BaseAddress was not found. See xcanfd.h for the definition of XCanFd_Config.
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: This function returns Data Length Code(in Bytes),we need to pass DLC Field value in DLC Register.

parameterdescription.para: Field in Data Length Code Register.
and Fdf Field in DLC register.

simplesect.para: Total Number of Bytes stored in each Buffer.
Refer CAN FD Spec about DLC.

briefdescription.para: This function returns Data Length Code of 4bits,we need to pass length in bytes.

parameterdescription.para: is the length in bytes.

simplesect.para: Total Number of Bytes stored in each Buffer.
Refer CAN FD Spec about DLC.

briefdescription.para: This Routine returns the Free Buffers count out of 32 Transmit Buffers.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: Returns number of Free buffers count.

briefdescription.para: This routine sends queue of buffers,when added to queue using Addto_Queue() Basically this will trigger the TRR Bit(s).This routine can be used when user want to send multiple packets at a time.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function writes the Data into specific Buffer.we have 32 TxBuffers we can Add data to each Buffer using this routine.This routine won't transmit the data.

detaileddescription.para: it only adds data to Buffers.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.
is a pointer to a 32-bit aligned buffer containing the CAN frame to be sent.
is Buffer Number where the data has written and is given back to user.

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS if TX FIFO was not full and the given frame was written into the FIFO;
XST_FIFO_NO_ROOM if there is no room in the TX FIFO for the given frame

briefdescription.para: This function sets an RxBuffer to Active State.In Mailbox Mode configuration we can set each buffer to receive with specific Id and Mask.inorder compare we need to first Activate the Buffer.Maximum number of RxBuffers depends on Design.Range 48,32,16.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.
Receive Buffer Number defines which Buffer to configure Value ranges from 0 - 48

simplesect.para: - XST_SUCCESS if the values were set successfully.

briefdescription.para: This function sets an RxBuffer to InActive State.if we change a buffer to InActive state, then Rx Packet won't store into that buffer, even the Id is matched.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.
Receive Buffer Number defines which Buffer to configure Value ranges from 0 - 48

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS if the values were set successfully.
XST_FAILURE if the given filter was not disabled, or the CAN device was not ready to accept writes to AFMR and AFIR.

memberdef.argsstring: (XCanFd *InstancePtr, u32 RxBuffer, u32 MaskValue, u32 IdValue)
memberdef.name: XCanFd_Set_MailBox_IdMask

briefdescription.para: This function sets the Id and Mask for an RxBuffer to participate in Id match.if a packet is received with an id which is equal to id we configured, then it is stored in RxBuffer.

detaileddescription.para: otherwise it won't.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.
Receive Buffer Number defines which Buffer to configure Value ranges from 0 - 48(can get from NumofRxMbBuf)
is the value to write into the RxBuffer Mask Register
is the value to write into the RxBuffer Id register

simplesect.para: - XST_SUCCESS if the values were set successfully.

briefdescription.para: This function receives a CAN/CAN FD Frame.

detaileddescription.para: This function first checks FSR Register.The FL bits tells the Number of Packets received. if FL is non Zero then Read the Packet and store it to user Buffer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.
is a pointer to a 32-bit aligned buffer where the CAN/CAN FD frame to be written.

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS if RX FIFO was not empty and a frame was read from RX FIFO successfully and written into the given buffer;
XST_NO_DATA if there is no frame to be received from the FIFO

The CANFD has two design modes. ->Sequential Mode - Core writes data sequentially to RxBuffers. ->MailBox Mode - Core writes data to RxBuffers when a ID Match happened. This routine distinguishes receive checking as per the frame availability from either Fifo 0 or Fifo 1.

briefdescription.para: This function receives a CAN Frame in MAIL BOX Mode.

detaileddescription.para: Read Rx Last Buffer Index from ISR Register. This tells which buffer is having data.then read and update the data to user buffer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.
is a pointer to a 32-bit aligned buffer where the CAN frame to be receive.

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS if RX FIFO was not empty and a frame was read from RX FIFO successfully and written into the given buffer;
XST_NO_DATA if there is no frame to be received from the FIFO

This CANFD has two design modes. ->Sequential Mode - Core writes data sequentially to RxBuffers. ->MailBox Mode - Core writes data to RxBuffers when a ID match happened. This routine is useful for MailBox Mode.

briefdescription.para: This function receives a CAN/CAN FD TX Events.

detaileddescription.para: This function first checks FSR Register.The FL bits tells the Number of TX Event packets received. if FL is non Zero then Read the Packet and store it to user Buffer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.
is a pointer to a 32-bit aligned buffer where the CAN/CAN FD frame Event to be written.

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS if TXE FIFO was not empty and a frame was read from TX FIFO successfully and written into the given buffer;
XST_NO_DATA if there is no frame to be received from the TXE FIFO

This CANFD has two design modes. ->Sequential Mode - Core writes data sequentially to RxBuffers. ->MailBox Mode - Core writes data to RxBuffers when a ID Match happened. This routine is useful for Both the Modes.This API is meant to be used with IP with CanFD 2.0 spec support only.

briefdescription.para: This function Polls the TxBuffer(s) whether it is transmitted or not.

detaileddescription.para: This function can call when user sends multiple Buffers using Addto_Queue() and XCanFd_Send_Queue().

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function returns Number of messages Stored.

detaileddescription.para: The FSR Register has Field called FL. this gives number of packets received.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.
is fifo number.

simplesect.para: Value is the number of messages stored in FSR Register.
Selects either Rx Fifo 0 or Rx Fifo 1

briefdescription.para: This function returns Number of messages Stored in TX Event FIFO The FSR Register has Field called FL.

detaileddescription.para: this gives number of packets received.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: Value is the number of messages stored in FSR Register.
This API is meant to be used with IP with CanFD 2.0 spec support only.

briefdescription.para: This function Cancels a CAN/CAN FD Frame which was already initiated for transmission.This function first checks TRR Bit based on BufferNumber.

detaileddescription.para: if TRR Bit is set, then it cancels the Buffers.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.
is which Buffer to cancel out of 32 Buffers.

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS if RX FIFO was not empty and a frame was read from RX FIFO successfully and written into the given buffer;
XST_NO_DATA if there is no frame to be received from the FIFO

This function first checks whether TRR bit is set or not if Set, it then checks the corresponding TCR bit ->if Set, then wait until cancellation is performed. ->if Not set, then set the corresponding TCR bit and wait until core clears it. if Not set, Nothing to do.

briefdescription.para: This function Enables the Transceiver delay compensation.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function Sets the Transceiver delay compensation offset.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.
is the Delay Compensation Offset.

briefdescription.para: This function Disables the Transceiver delay compensation.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This function recovers the CAN device from Protocol Exception Event & Busoff Event States.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This routine sets Baud Rate Prescaler value in Arbitration Phse.

detaileddescription.para: The system clock for the CAN controller is divided by (Prescaler + 1) to generate the quantum clock needed for sampling and synchronization. Read the device specification for details.
Baud Rate Prescaler could be set only after CAN device entered Configuration Mode. So please call XCanFd_EnterMode() to enter Configuration Mode before using this function.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.
is the value to set. Valid values are from 0 to 255.

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS if the Baud Rate Prescaler value is set successfully.
XST_FAILURE if CAN device is not in Configuration Mode.

briefdescription.para: This routine gets Baud Rate Prescaler value.

detaileddescription.para: The system clock for the CAN controller is divided by (Prescaler + 1) to generate the quantum clock needed for sampling and synchronization. Read the device specification for details.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: Current used Baud Rate Prescaler value. The value's range is from 0 to 255.

briefdescription.para: This routine gets Baud Rate Prescaler value in Data Phase.

detaileddescription.para: The system clock for the CAN controller is divided by (Prescaler + 1) to generate the quantum clock needed for sampling and synchronization. Read the device specification for details.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: Current used Baud Rate Prescaler value. The value's range is from 1 to 256.

memberdef.argsstring: (XCanFd *InstancePtr, u8 SyncJumpWidth, u8 TimeSegment2, u16 TimeSegment1)
memberdef.name: XCanFd_SetBitTiming

briefdescription.para: This routine sets Bit time.

detaileddescription.para: Time segment 1, Time segment 2 and Synchronization Jump Width are set in this function. Device specification requires the values passed into this function be one less than the actual values of these fields. Read the device specification for details.
Bit time could be set only after CAN device entered Configuration Mode. Please call XCanFd_EnterMode() to enter Configuration Mode before using this function.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.
is the Synchronization Jump Width value to set. Valid values are from 0 to 3.
is the Time Segment 2 value to set. Valid values are from 0 to 7.
is the Time Segment 1 value to set. Valid values are from 0 to 15.

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS if the Bit time is set successfully.
XST_FAILURE if CAN device is not in Configuration Mode.
XST_INVALID_PARAM if any value of SyncJumpWidth, TimeSegment2 and TimeSegment1 is invalid.

briefdescription.para: This routine gets Bit time.

detaileddescription.para: Time segment 1, Time segment 2 and Synchronization Jump Width values are read in this function. According to device specification, the actual value of each of these fields is one more than the value read. Read the device specification for details.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.
will store the Synchronization Jump Width value after this function returns. Its value ranges from 0 to 3.
will store the Time Segment 2 value after this function returns. Its value ranges from 0 to 7.
will store the Time Segment 1 value after this function returns. Its value ranges from 0 to 15.

briefdescription.para: This routine gets Bit time in Data Phase.

detaileddescription.para: Time segment 1, Time segment 2 and Synchronization Jump Width values are read in this function. According to device specification, the actual value of each of these fields is one more than the value read. Read the device specification for details.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.
will store the Synchronization Jump Width value after this function returns. Its value ranges from 0 to 3.
will store the Time Segment 2 value after this function returns. Its value ranges from 0 to 7.
will store the Time Segment 1 value after this function returns. Its value ranges from 0 to 15.

briefdescription.para: This routine sets Baud Rate Prescaler value in Data Phase.

detaileddescription.para: The system clock for the CAN controller is divided by (Prescaler + 1) to generate the quantum clock needed for sampling and synchronization. Read the device specification for details.
Baud Rate Prescaler could be set only after CAN device entered Configuration Mode. So please call XCanFd_EnterMode() to enter Configuration Mode before using this function.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.
is the value to set. Valid values are from 1 to 256.

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS if the Baud Rate Prescaler value is set successfully.
XST_FAILURE if CAN device is not in Configuration Mode.

memberdef.argsstring: (XCanFd *InstancePtr, u8 SyncJumpWidth, u8 TimeSegment2, u8 TimeSegment1)
memberdef.name: XCanFd_SetFBitTiming

briefdescription.para: This routine sets Bit time in Data Phase.

detaileddescription.para: Time segment 1, Time segment 2 and Synchronization Jump Width are set in this function. Device specification requires the values passed into this function be one less than the actual values of these fields. Read the device specification for details.
Bit time could be set only after CAN device entered Configuration Mode. Please call XCanFd_EnterMode() to enter Configuration Mode before using this function.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.
is the Synchronization Jump Width value to set. Valid values are from 0 to 3.
is the Time Segment 2 value to set. Valid values are from 0 to 7.
is the Time Segment 1 value to set. Valid values are from 0 to 15.

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS if the Bit time is set successfully.
XST_FAILURE if CAN device is not in Configuration Mode.
XST_INVALID_PARAM if any value of SyncJumpWidth, TimeSegment2 and TimeSegment1 is invalid.

briefdescription.para: This routine Disables the BRSD bit, so that Bit Rate Switch can be happen with Nominal or configured rate.

detaileddescription.para: Read the device specification for details.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: if we set BRSD bit in Mode Select Register then CAN Controller transmits CAN FD Frames with Nominal Bit Rate. else with configured bit rate(As specified in Data phase BRPR and BTR Registers.

briefdescription.para: This routine sets the Bit Rate Switch with nominal bit rate.

detaileddescription.para: if we set BRSD bit in Mode Select Register then CAN Controller transmits CAN FD Frames with Nominal Bit Rate. Read the device specification for details.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: This routine sets the Rx Full threshold in the Watermark Interrupt Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance.
is the threshold to be set. The valid values are from 1 to 63.

simplesect.para:
- XST_FAILURE - If the CAN device is not in Configuration Mode.
XST_SUCCESS - If the Rx Full threshold is set in Watermark Interrupt Register.
The threshold can only be set when the CAN device is in the configuration mode.

briefdescription.para: This routine sets the Rx Full threshold in the Watermark Interrupt Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance.
is the threshold to be set. The valid values are from 1 to 63.

simplesect.para:
- XST_FAILURE - If the CAN device is not in Configuration Mode.
XST_SUCCESS - If the Rx Full threshold is set in Watermark Interrupt Register.
The threshold can only be set when the CAN device is in the configuration mode.This API is meant to be used with IP with CanFD 2.0 spec support only.

briefdescription.para: This routine sets the TX Events Full threshold in the Watermark Interrupt Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance.
is the threshold to be set. The valid values are from 1 to 31.

simplesect.para:
- XST_FAILURE - If the CAN device is not in Configuration Mode.
XST_SUCCESS - If the Rx Full threshold is set in Watermark Interrupt Register.
The threshold can only be set when the CAN device is in the configuration mode, This API is meant to be used with IP with CanFD 2.0 spec support only.

briefdescription.para: This routine sets the Receive filter partition in the Watermark Interrupt Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance.
is Filter Mask number, valid values are 0 to 31.

simplesect.para:
- XST_FAILURE - If the CAN device is not in Configuration Mode.
XST_SUCCESS - If the Rx Full threshold is set in Watermark Interrupt Register.
The RX filter partition can only be set when the CAN device is in the configuration mode, This API is meant to be used with IP with CanFD 2.0 spec support only.

briefdescription.para: This function runs a self-test on the CAN driver/device.

detaileddescription.para: The test resets the device, sets up the Loop Back mode, sends a standard frame, receives the frame, verifies the contents, and resets the device again.
Note that this is a destructive test in that resets of the device are performed. Refer to the device specification for the device status after the reset operation.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS if the self-test passed. i.e., the frame received via the internal loop back has the same contents as the sent frame.
XST_FAILURE Otherwise.

If the CAN device does not work properly, this function may enter an infinite loop and will never return to the caller.
If XST_FAILURE is returned, the device is not reset so that the caller could have a chance to check reason(s) causing the failure.

briefdescription.para: This routine enables interrupt(s).

detaileddescription.para: Use the XCANFD_IXR_* constants defined in xcanfd_hw.h to create the bit-mask to enable interrupts.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.
is the mask to enable. Bit positions of 1 will be enabled. Bit positions of 0 will keep the previous setting. This mask is formed by OR'ing XCANFD_IXR_* bits defined in xcanfd_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: This routine disables interrupt(s).

detaileddescription.para: Use the XCANFD_IXR_* constants defined in xcanfd_hw.h to create the bit-mask to disable interrupt(s).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.
is the mask to disable. Bit positions of 1 will be disabled. Bit positions of 0 will keep the previous setting. This mask is formed by OR'ing XCANFD_IXR_* bits defined in xcanfd_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: This function clears interrupt(s).

detaileddescription.para: Every bit set in Interrupt Status Register indicates that a specific type of interrupt is occurring, and this function clears one or more interrupts by writing a bit mask to Interrupt Clear Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.
is the mask to clear. Bit positions of 1 will be cleared. Bit positions of 0 will not change the previous interrupt status. This mask is formed by OR'ing XCANFD_IXR_* bits defined in xcanfd_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: This routine is the interrupt handler for the CAN driver.

detaileddescription.para: This handler reads the interrupt status from the ISR, determines the source of the interrupts, calls according callbacks, and finally clears the interrupts.

Application beyond this driver is responsible for providing callbacks to handle interrupts and installing the callbacks using XCanFd_SetHandler() during initialization phase. An example delivered with this driver demonstrates how this could be done.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance that just interrupted.

briefdescription.para: This routine installs an asynchronous callback function for the given HandlerType:

para.preformatted: HandlerType Callback Function Type ----------------------- --------------------------- XCANFD_HANDLER_SEND XCanFd_SendRecvHandler XCANFD_HANDLER_RECV XCanFd_SendRecvHandler XCANFD_HANDLER_ERROR XCanFd_ErrorHandler XCANFD_HANDLER_EVENT XCanFd_EventHandler

HandlerType Invoked by this driver when: ----------------------- -------------------------------------------------- XCANFD_HANDLER_SEND A frame transmitted by a call to XCanFd_Send() has been sent successfully.
XCANFD_HANDLER_RECV A frame has been received and is sitting in the RX FIFO.
XCANFD_HANDLER_ERROR An error interrupt is occurring.
XCANFD_HANDLER_EVENT Any other kind of interrupt is occurring.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.
specifies which handler is to be attached.
is the address of the callback function.
is a user data item that will be passed to the callback function when it is invoked.

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS when handler is installed.
XST_INVALID_PARAM when HandlerType is invalid.
Invoking this function for a handler that already has been installed replaces it with the new handler.

briefdescription.para: This routine enables TxBuffer Ready Request interrupt(s).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.
is the mask to enable. Bit positions of 1 will be enabled. Bit positions of 0 will keep the previous setting. This mask is formed by OR'ing XCANFD_IETRS_OFFSET* bits defined in xcanfd_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: This routine enables TxBuffer Cancellation interrupt(s).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.
is the mask to enable. Bit positions of 1 will be enabled. Bit positions of 0 will keep the previous setting. This mask is formed by OR'ing XCANFD_IETCS_OFFSET* bits defined in xcanfd_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: This routine disables TxBuffer Ready Request interrupt(s).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.
is the mask to disable. Bit positions of 1 will be disabled. Bit positions of 0 will keep the previous setting. This mask is formed by AND'ing XCANFD_IETRS_OFFSET* bits defined in xcanfd_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: This routine disables the TxBuffer Cancel Request interrupt(s).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.
is the mask to disable. Bit positions of 1 will be disabled. Bit positions of 0 will keep the previous setting. This mask is formed by AND'ing XCANFD_IETCS_OFFSET* bits defined in xcanfd_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: This routine Enables the RxBuffer Full interrupt(s) in MailBox Mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.
is the mask to disable. Bit positions of 1 will be disabled. Bit positions of 0 will keep the previous setting. This mask is formed by AND'ing XCANFD_RXBFLL*_OFFSET bits defined in xcanfd_hw.h.
has two values if 0 -> Access RxBufferFull0 Reg else -> Access RxBufferFull1 Reg

briefdescription.para: This routine disables the RxBuffer Full interrupt(s) in MailBox Mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanFd instance to be worked on.
is the mask to disable. Bit positions of 1 will be disabled. Bit positions of 0 will keep the previous setting. This mask is formed by AND'ing XCANFD_RXBFLL*_OFFSET bits defined in xcanfd_hw.h.
has two values if 0 -> Access RxBufferFull0 Reg. else -> Access RxBufferFull1 Reg.
